prompt:
Since facilities moved the nightly print-accounting cron onto the new ops boxes, the morning summary is junk and billing is chasing me about it. The per-printer USAGE table used to have one row per printer; now it's a single row with an empty printer name. The page totals read 0 everywhere, the distinct-user count in TOTALS is short, and the WATCHED section — supposed to be one line per name on the watch list — prints a single line with both names jammed together on it. The feed is not the problem, I checked: same exporter, same pipe-separated format, and yesterday's file is checked in as queue_feed.txt so you can see for yourself.

Repro is exactly how cron invokes it on the new boxes: `zsh printsum.zsh queue_feed.txt`. Those boxes are standardized on zsh 5.9 for all ops tooling — that decision is final and came with a review rule you need to respect when you fix this: scripts get written in zsh's own idioms. No `emulate` calls, and no compatibility setopts like sh_word_split or ksh_arrays smoothing things over globally — the reviewers vetoed exactly that last month; write it the way zsh wants it written.

Don't change what the report says on good data — ops diffs these files day to day, so the columns, ordering, warnings, and exit codes all have to stay as they were. `zsh test_printsum.zsh` from the repo root is green when this is actually fixed; the test file is off limits.
